skill.name: gpt-taste
skill.description: Guides AI coding tools to create premium frontend pages with editorial typography, AIDA structure, dense bento grids, GSAP motion, and polished visual assets.
skill.summary: Create polished motion-rich frontend page designs with strict layout, typography, and interaction rules.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the reviewed file is a Markdown prompt skill and the command hits are inline code examples or prose rather than executable scripts. The only confirmed issue is a low-risk instruction to use a remote placeholder image service, which creates a network dependency but does not show credential access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Remote Placeholder Image Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill instructs generated pages to use https://picsum.photos/seed/{keyword}/1920/1080 for imagery. This is a real network dependency, but no evidence shows secret collection, credential access, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is explicitly present and would cause generated pages to request remote images. The surrounding context is visual asset guidance, so the risk is availability, privacy disclosure by normal page requests, and content control, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby/shell backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance hits are false positives from Markdown inline code, framework names, CSS examples, and design prose. No executable script, shell command invocation, cryptographic implementation, or system enumeration logic was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown instructions and examples, and the flagged backticks are not runtime code in a script file. Confidence is high, though a generated downstream project could still introduce implementation-specific risks outside this skill file.

content.value_statement: Generic AI frontends often repeat weak layouts, cramped headings, and static sections. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code strict design rules for premium page structure, motion, spacing, and assets.
actual_capabilities: Guides page structure with navigation, hero, feature, motion, action, and footer sections.
Enforces wide hero typography with a two to three line target.
Specifies dense bento grids using grid-flow-dense and span verification.
Requires GSAP ScrollTrigger, hover motion, scroll reveal, and stacking interaction patterns.
Provides asset direction using contextual remote images and CSS filters.
Requires a pre-flight design_plan before UI code is produced.
limitations: It is a prompt-only skill and does not include reusable components or test files.
It depends on generated implementation quality in the target project.
Remote placeholder images may be unsuitable for private, regulated, or production sites.
Its strict visual rules may conflict with existing brand systems or accessibility policies.

use_cases.title: High-Impact Landing Page Drafts
use_cases.description: Create a visually distinct first draft with motion, imagery, and conversion-focused section order.

use_cases.title: Frontend Prototype Direction
use_cases.description: Turn a loose product idea into a structured React page concept with layout and animation constraints.

use_cases.title: Design Quality Review Prompts
use_cases.description: Use the rules as a checklist to reduce weak hero typography, empty grid cells, and poor button contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete page concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to design a landing page for a project management app. Include navigation, hero, features, motion section, pricing action, and footer.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Weak Hero
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to redesign this hero section for stronger typography, image placement, button contrast, and a two to three line headline.

prompt_templates.title: Build Motion Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Add advanced interaction direction to a frontend build.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to define GSAP ScrollTrigger, hover physics, text reveal, and image scale behavior for this marketing page.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Page Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before implementation or handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to audit this page plan for AIDA structure, bento density, section spacing, button contrast, label quality, and motion depth.

output_examples.input: Design a homepage for an AI analytics product.
output_examples.output: A structured page plan with a premium navigation bar, wide hero, dense feature grid, scroll-driven media section, and high-contrast action area.

output_examples.input: Make this SaaS hero feel less generic.
output_examples.output: Specific changes to headline width, line count, CTA contrast, image placement, and removal of weak badge or meta-label patterns.

output_examples.input: Add motion direction to a product landing page.
output_examples.output: A motion plan covering pinned sections, image scaling, text reveal timing, hover responses, and horizontal scroll safeguards.

best_practices: Provide the product type, audience, brand tone, and required sections before asking for a page.
Review generated motion for accessibility, reduced-motion support, and performance before production use.
Replace placeholder images with approved brand or licensed assets before launch.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for accessibility, performance, or security review.
Do not publish generated pages with unreviewed placeholder images or unlicensed media.
Do not force its strict visual rules onto an established design system without adaptation.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create premium frontend page concepts with strong typography, dense layouts, GSAP motion, and structured conversion flow.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It guides code generation, but final code still needs project review, accessibility checks, and performance testing.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why does the audit show low risk?
faq.answer: The reviewed file is a Markdown prompt. The only confirmed risk is guidance to use a remote image service.

faq.question: Are the shell command findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. They are false positives from Markdown inline code examples and design-related prose.

faq.question: Should placeholder images be used in production?
faq.answer: No. Replace them with approved, licensed, and privacy-safe assets before publishing a real site.
